drafted_for_review.summary: Prioritized P0/P1/P2/P3 list of remaining v0 work. P0 = Phase 14 streaming bug + phase11_smoke regression. P1 = Plan 12-10 push-and-get + Phase 12-01..06 follow-up + Phase 14.1. P2 = Phase 13 Hetzner sweep with shard scaling + docs/packaging + metric wiring. P3 = post-v0 polish.

key_questions_user_asked_this_session.q: Why is Hetzner box performance so low?
key_questions_user_asked_this_session.a: Three factors: (1) Apple-M4's per-core single-thread perf is ~2× older EPYC KVM cores; Beava's apply is single-threaded so single-thread perf dominates. (2) KVM virtualization overhead ~10-15%. (3) Bench-side glibc mmap_lock contention hits 19% of CPU at saturated push (not server-side issue). Recommendation: use Hetzner CCX (dedicated cores) or AWS Graviton3 c7g.4xlarge for Phase 13 sweep.

key_questions_user_asked_this_session.q: Can we do shard benchmark on 16 CPU?
key_questions_user_asked_this_session.a: Yes — recommended as part of Phase 13 Hetzner sweep. Test 1/2/4/8 Beava instances, each on different ports, key%N sharded at bench-side, aggregate sustained_eps. Validates project_no_sharded_apply commitment. ~1-2 hours implementation + ~2-4 hours data. Strong v0 marketing data point if scaling is near-linear.

key_questions_user_asked_this_session.q: Are we good on read/write throughput? Should we focus on correctness?
key_questions_user_asked_this_session.a: YES, pivot to correctness. Reads (210k r/s fraud-team) + writes (110k EPS fraud-team / 700k EPS small) are competitive. Apply is at 100% efficiency for fraud-team push (architectural ceiling). Layer 1-2 fixes give 1.5-2× more on reads but are diminishing returns. The remaining v0 work is correctness (Phase 14 streaming bug), functional completeness (Plan 12-10 push-and-get + 12-01..06 follow-up), and ship-readiness (Phase 13 docs/packaging).
